description_long: This is a maroon and yellow diesel locomotive with the number 56074 and the EWS (English, Welsh & Scottish Railway) logo prominently displayed on its sides. The body is rectangular with a slightly rounded roof, featuring two large yellow insignias on top. The sides have a series of ventilation grilles and small, evenly spaced rectangular windows near the front. The front and rear ends are flat with visible couplers, lights, and buffer details, and the undercarriage is equipped with black wheels and suspension components. The exterior appears to be made primarily of metal, with painted surfaces and some plastic or glass elements for windows and lights. This locomotive is designed for hauling freight or passenger carriages and is typically found on urban and suburban rail networks.
description: A maroon and yellow EWS diesel locomotive with detailed side grilles, small windows, and prominent logos, designed for urban rail use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face, headlights, coupler, and buffer details, with the maroon roof and yellow insignias visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the EWS logo, number 56074, ventilation grilles, and small front windows, with the full length of the locomotive visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the flat end, coupler, and buffer details, with the maroon roof and insignias.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with EWS logo, number, grilles, and small windows, showing the locomotive's length.
attribute_window_arrangement: spaced small rectangular windows near the front
